parsed_review.summary: CP00-305 is a descriptor-only RP09 CRM "UI binding slice" pack (Risk A, 10 units RP09.P04.M05.S06-S15, micro M05 "Permission And Audit Binding") that faithfully follows the validated per-pack template established across CP00-299..CP00-304. Source inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms identical CP00-305 values threaded through pack binding, requirements, the service.js factories, the crm-core-contract.json projection, validators, tests, the rp09 validate script, README, manifest, and command-evidence. The deliverable distribution (claude_review:1, ui:4, security_audit:2, implementation:3) and micro_title distribution ("Permission And Audit Binding":10) match the manifest/baseline. All no-write/no-real-data/no-runtime boundaries are asserted and validator-enforced, including deny-over-allow and per-row no-leak guards on permission decisions, audit hints, and validation errors. The authority boundary is correct: Claude is a read-only reviewer (allowed tools Read/Grep/Glob), claude_is_final_approval=false, enterprise trust is not claimed from local validation, and human approval is required to open runtime. The pack does not prematurely claim production_ready (manifest production_ready=false, status implemented_pending_review, review status pending); the only "production_ready" signal is the Hermes "production_ready_candidate" flag, which is appropriate at this stage. Hermes patterns are used only for the deterministic review packet/receipt, not product runtime (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false). All six prior-pack drift allow-lists include CP305, the readiness auth preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON (1436 bytes), and recorded command-evidence shows every implementation, contract, plan, and repo-wide validation command exiting 0. No P0/P1/P2 issues found. One cosmetic P3: the generated changed-file-scope.json over-lists the pack's own evidence dir and the stray new-session-handoff.md as "unrelated_dirty_files_preserved", but the authoritative manifest worktree policy scopes them correctly and no active untracked file is wrongly omitted, so there is no scope leak. This is an independent read-only review and not a final approval; human final approval remains required for any enterprise trust claim.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json over-lists pack-owned and stray docs under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved
findings.details: The generated review-scope file lists the pack's own evidence directory 'docs/closeout-packs/cp00-305/' and the pre-existing stray 'docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md' inside unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, and additionally lists new-session-handoff.md under untracked_implementation_files. Labeling the pack's own output dir 'unrelated' is semantically imprecise (it is the active pack evidence dir). This is a quirk of how the scope generator buckets untracked files (active_pack_implementation_refs only enumerates tracked source files). The authoritative manifest worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved correctly lists only the truly-unrelated files (the v2.0 .docx and the c-ldip review-results JSON), and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted / active_pack_required_untracked_files are empty, so there is no actual scope leak, no commit-scope risk, and no boundary violation.
findings.recommendation: Optional: refine the changed-file-scope generator so the active pack's own docs/closeout-packs/<pack>/ evidence dir is classified as active pack scope rather than 'unrelated', and reconcile the dual listing of new-session-handoff.md. No change to pack implementation or commit scope is required; non-blocking.
